intended_use: JLK-ICH is a radiological computer-aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of non-contrast CT images. JLK-ICH is a notification-only, parallel workflow tool that is in
intended_use_full: JLK-ICH is a radiological computer-aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of non-contrast CT images. JLK-ICH is a notification-only, parallel workflow tool that is intended to assist hospital networks and trained clinicians to identify and communicate images of specific patients to specialists, independent of the standard of care workflow. JLK-ICH uses an artificial intelligence algorithm to analyze images for findings suggestive of pre-specified clinical conditions and promptly notifies the appropriate medical specialists of these findings in parallel with the standard of care image interpretation. Identification of suspected findings is not for diagnostic use beyond notification. Specifically, the device analyzes non-contrast CT images of the head to detect intracranial hemorrhage (ICH).
target_condition: Intracranial hemorrhage (ICH) including intraparenchymal, intraventricular, subarachnoid, subdural, and epidural hemorrhage
ground_truth: Ground truth determined by two American Board of Radiologists (ABR)-certified neuroradiologists, with consensus reached by a third in case of disagreement (2+1 truther scheme)
other_metrics: NCCT-to-notification time: 0.19 ± 0.04 minutes (mean 0.19, 95% CI 0.186–0.197); Performance stratified by age, gender, race-ethnicity, scanner manufacturer, slice thickness, ICH subtype, and ICH volume
